{{Quote|Well that's my cue! Best to father and send my love to Lieda. Tell her I'll write as soon as I can|Jobin|<ref name="E" />}}
'''Lieda Mothma''' was the [[Gender/Legends|daughter]] of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] and the sister of [[Jobin]]. Mon wished that she could dedicate her [[Time/Legends|time]] to Lieda, but she was steadfast in her commitment to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. After the foundation of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Lieda spent time convalescing on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]]. She later resided with her mother on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where Mon [[Death/Legends|passed away]] in [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]].

==Biography==
[[File:MonMothma-RotJAVA.png|thumb|right|250px|Lieda Mothma was the daughter of Mon Mothma (pictured).]]
Lieda Mothma<ref name="CSWE" /> was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] to the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Chandrilan/Legends|Chandrilan]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]] and the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], of which Mon was a founder.<ref name="NEGC" /> A member of the [[Mothma family/Legends|Mothma family]], Lieda had a Human brother named [[Jobin]]<ref name="CSWE" /> and was the granddaughter of [[Tanis Mothma]], [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of [[Hanna City/Legends|Hanna City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]],<ref name="Coruscant">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref> and her [[Mon Mothma's father/Legends|spouse]], a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Arbiter-general/Legends|arbiter-general]].<ref name="Mon Mothma databank">{{DB|character|monmothma|Mothma, Mon}}</ref> Having a daughter did not slow Mon's aspirations for the Rebellion against the Empire down,<ref name="NEGC" /> although she wished she could retire from service and devote herself to Lieda.<ref name="DES" />

Just as the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] commenced<ref name="E" /> in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Jobin created a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] message to his mother in which he told Mon to send Lieda his love and inform her that he would write as soon as he could. Jobin was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial forces]] in the battle, and his message did not reach his mother until<ref name="E" /> [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC Endor">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> as she prepared to deliver a briefing for the upcoming [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="E" />

[[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was killed in the battle, and Mon became [[Chief Councilor]] of the Empire's successor state: the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="NEGC" /> Sometime afterward, Lieda was in convalescence on the planet [[New Alderaan/Legends|New Alderaan]], where she was the only person who saw past Mon's confident surface and recognized how much her years of service had cost her, although Lieda also saw a new resolve in her mother.<ref name="DES" /> After the [[Almanian Uprising]]<ref name="NEGC" /> in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Lieda joined her mother in Mon's main home on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to have a quiet life<ref name="NEGC" /> until Mon's peaceful death in [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Of note is that Lieda has never been mentioned as Lieda Mothma, thus opening up the possibility that she was born outside of wedlock.|Pablo Hidalgo, responding to a fan question about Lieda in 2005 prior to her surname being confirmed as Mothma|<ref name="Insider 82">{{InsiderCite|82|Ask Lobot|Ask the Master}}</ref>}}
In the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, Mon Mothma's daughter was first mentioned in the ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', written by [[Michael Allen Horne]] and published in [[1993]] by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="DES" /> Her first name of Lieda was confirmed in the [[2002]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="NEGC" />

[[File:Leida-Andor5.png|thumb|right|250px|While Lieda Mothma never made a full appearance in a Legends story, the new ''Star Wars'' canon introduced Leida Mothma (pictured) in the streaming series ''Andor''.]]
In the "[[Ask Lobot|Ask the Master]]" section of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 82|82nd issue]],<ref name="Insider 82" /> which began shipping to subscribers on [[May 17]], [[2005]],<ref name="Insider date">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20050506.html|text=The Dark Side Shines in ''Star Wars Insider'' #82}}</ref> author [[Pablo Hidalgo]] was asked about Lieda Mothma and who her father was. Hidalgo described Lieda as a mysterious character and commented that the name Lieda Mothma had not yet been used [[canon]]ically. He stated that this information made room for the possibility that Lieda was born out of wedlock, as no source had confirmed whether Mon had [[Marriage/Legends|married]].<ref name="Insider 82" /> The name Lieda Mothma was then confirmed in her brother Jobin's entry in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE" />

[[Leida Mothma]], a version of the character with the spelling of her first name altered, debuted in the new ''[[Star Wars]]'' canon in "[[The Axe Forgets]]," the fifth episode of the [[Disney+]] series ''[[Star Wars: Andor]]''{{'s}} [[Andor Season 1|first season]],<ref name="Andor">{{Andor|The Axe Forgets}}</ref> which premiered on [[October 5]], [[2022]].<ref name="Release dates">{{Twitter|starwars|status/1557442379975573504|[[Star Wars]]|date=2022-8-10|quote=This is what revolution looks like. Watch #Andor, a Star Wars Original Series, streaming only on @DisneyPlus September 21.|archivedate=20220810191942}}</ref> She was portrayed by actress [[Bronte Carmichael]].<ref name="Andor" /> The accompanying episode guide published on [[StarWars.com]] acknowledged the character's origins in the ''Dark Empire Sourcebook'' and the fact that she had not been previously featured in any story.<ref name="Andor guide">{{SW|url=andor-s1-e5-trivia-gallery|text="The Axe Forgets" Trivia Gallery | Andor}} {{C|Slide 4}}</ref>
